title: Layer models from staging to mart
description: Separate light source-shaped staging models from business-shaped mart models so logic is reusable and lineage is clear.
body: Use when structuring a dbt project beyond a handful of models. Do not pile joins, renames, and business logic into one giant model — it becomes untestable and unreusable. Actions: build staging models that rename and lightly clean each source one-to-one, intermediate models for reusable joins, and mart models shaped for consumption; keep each layer's responsibility distinct. Validation signals include a clean staging-to-mart lineage graph and mart models reusing tested staging models rather than raw sources. Risk boundary: skipping layers couples business logic to raw source quirks; if a mart reads raw sources directly, treat it as technical debt and refactor through staging before it spreads.
